business.headline: Rosneft CEO predicts India will drive 50% of global oil demand growth by 2035Rosneft CEO cited International Energy Agency estimates projecting that India's oil consumption would reach nearly eight million barrels per day by 2035, representing growth of 44 per cent, while global demand overall would increase by about five per cent.

Indians have no will for a WILLA house. A few bank accounts. Some investments. Perhaps a family business. For many Indians, these assets represent decades of hard work. Yet surprisingly few leave behind clear instructions on who gets what, creating uncertainty for the very people they wanted to protect.

Oil India reports second gas discovery in Andaman offshore blockOil India has reported natural gas in the Vijayapuram-3 exploratory well in the Andaman offshore basin. The find adds to evidence of hydrocarbon potential in the block as appraisal work continues.

India-US trade deal's first tranche likely by mid-July, says Piyush GoyalSpeaking about the discussions, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said the meetings held between June 2 and June 4 were very encouraging.

India's economy grows 7.7% in FY26 despite global turmoil, war concernsProvisional government estimates show economic growth accelerated from the previous year, with robust expansion in the January-March quarter helping lift full-year GDP growth to 7.7%.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as RBI holds rates; rupee logs biggest gain since AprilMarkets ended lower after the RBI kept the repo rate at 5.25%, while the rupee posted its biggest one-day gain since April. Investors are now weighing inflation, crude prices, foreign outflows and West Asia tensions for clues on market direction.

Gold, FDs or real estate? I asked experts where to invest during rupee weaknessWhen the rupee comes under pressure, many investors start looking for a safe haven. But which asset actually delivers—gold, FDs or real estate? I spoke to experts to find out.

Before Sebi stepped in, Rajesh Exports had already raised eyebrows for yearsSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports has brought fresh scrutiny to its reported revenues and subsidiary disclosures. The action has revived years of public questions over how the company matched vast turnover with thin profits.

The hidden inflation nobody is talking about: Transport inflationAs transportation costs increase across the supply chain, businesses eventually pass those higher expenses on to consumers. The result is 'invisible inflation' – a force that pushes up the cost of almost everything but is rarely talked about on its own.

RBI MPC meeting: 7 key takeaways on repo rate, inflation, growth and moreThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced its latest monetary policy decisions, keeping the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and maintaining a neutral stance. Here are seven key takeaways from the RBI's June MPC meeting.

Rajesh Exports shares hit 5% lower circuit for 2nd straight day after Sebi orderRajesh Exports shares hit the 5% lower circuit for a second straight session after Sebi's interim order. The sell-off reflects investor caution as the regulator examines alleged revenue inflation and orders a fresh forensic audit.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: What it means for your home loan and EMIFor millions of home loan borrowers, the RBI's repo rate decision is closely watched because it can influence borrowing costs. With the central bank keeping the repo rate steady at 5.25%, should you expect any change in your monthly EMI?

RBI rolls out multiple measures to attract more dollars amid rupee weaknessThe RBI has unveiled a package of bond, swap and deposit measures to draw more dollars into India while keeping the repo rate unchanged. The steps are aimed at easing foreign currency liquidity strains as oil prices rise and investor outflows weigh on the rupee.

Higher crude prices forcing a rethink on inflation and growth outlook, says RBIThe RBI kept the repo rate unchanged as Sanjay Malhotra warned that the West Asia conflict is lifting oil prices and disrupting supplies. The central bank has cut its growth forecast and raised its inflation outlook as cost pressures begin to build.

Centre exempts foreign investors from capital gains tax on govt bondsThe tax break is aimed at boosting foreign investment in government bonds, improving post-tax returns for overseas investors and helping attract more stable capital flows into India.

RBI keeps repo rate steady at 5.25%, reduces GDP growth projection for FY27All six members of the MPC voted unanimously to maintain the policy repo rate at 5.25%. The central bank also retained its "neutral" policy stance, signalling that future decisions will depend on incoming economic data and evolving global developments.

PwC review flags accounting irregularities in IndusInd Bank treasury operationsA confidential PwC review reportedly found that manual treasury entries at IndusInd Bank offset trading losses and created a Rs 2,201.76 crore receivable pool. The findings have raised fresh questions over hedging links, accounting treatment and controls around manual adjustments.

Markets open higher as investors await RBI rate decision; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 191.96 points, or 0.26%, to 74,551.97 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 39 points, or 0.17%, to 23,455.55.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall or rise on RBI MPC decision?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,565.00 at 8:17 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 could open above its previous close of 23,416.55.

Gujarat wire-free cities plan: Urban power lines to go underground by 2030Gujarat has approved a statewide mission to move urban power lines underground by 2030. The phased project aims to improve power reliability and safety while making cities less cluttered.

entertainment.headline: Peddi row: Ratna Pathak's take on privileged women and objectification goes viral Amid the ongoing Peddi controversy over the portrayal of Janhvi Kapoor's character, an old video of Ratna Pathak discussing objectification in cinema has resurfaced and gone viral.

Aishwarya Rai, Akshaye Khanna's Taal dance clip goes viral after 25 years. Watch A 1999 video of Aishwarya Rai and Akshaye Khanna performing to Taal Se Taal Mila in Miami has resurfaced online and gone viral.

Madhoo recalls Nana Patekar's unexpected slap on Yeshwant sets: I hit him back Madhoo Shah recalled an intense moment on the sets of Yeshwant, revealing that she slapped Nana Patekar back after he unexpectedly hit her during an emotional scene.

Sunny Deol showers love on Bobby for Bandar success; shares pic with mother Prakash Sunny Deol shared a rare photograph of Bobby Deol with their mother Prakash Kaur after Bandar won praise. The post highlighted the family's pride as Bobby's performance drew wide appreciation.

Did Janhvi Kapoor flag concerns about her Peddi role? Leaked chats go viral Alleged leaked chats circulated by Janhvi Kapoor's fan clubs suggest that the actor had raised concerns about camera angles and the portrayal of her character in Peddi long before the film came under public scrutiny.

Shilpa Shinde reacts to criticism over recent remarks, says do whatever you can Shilpa Shinde has shared cryptic social media posts amid backlash over her admission that she made a false sexual harassment allegation during her Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain! dispute.

Chinese actor Jin Ze dies at 33; agency appeals for privacy, warns against rumours Actor Jin Ze, known for popular Chinese romance dramas such as Begin Again and Legally Romance, died at the age of 33.

AR Rahman to perform at Attari Border in a first-ever tribute to BSF bravehearts Music composer AR Rahman is set for a unique appearance at the Attari Border as part of a special event linked to Main Vaapas Aaunga. Here's what makes the tribute significant.

Director Advait Chandan exits Dostana 2? Here's what we know Advait Chandan has reportedly exited Dostana 2 after months of development. The latest setback leaves Dharma Productions' long-delayed sequel facing fresh uncertainty.

Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai box office Day 1: Varun takes early lead at box office Varun Dhawan's latest romantic comedy has opened ahead of its box-office rivals. Here's how Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai performed on its first day and what the early numbers reveal.

Meet Anjali Kulthe: 26/11 hero who saved 20 pregnant women, inspired Kangana's film As Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata brings a little-known 26/11 story to the big screen, the film shines a light on a woman whose courage helped save lives during one of India's darkest nights.

Peddi box office Day 2: Ram Charan starrer keeps buzz alive after record opening Ram Charan's Peddi crossed a major worldwide milestone in just two days, but its second-day box office trend tells a more nuanced story. Here's how the film performed across markets.

No intention to objectify: Peddi director apologises amid Janhvi's role row Filmmaker Buchi Babu Sana apologised and responded to criticism over Janhvi Kapoor's portrayal in Peddi, addressing audience backlash. He also hinted at key changes in the Ram Charan-starrer.

Deepika-Ranveer's Bollywood takeover: They aren't in the news, they are the news From Deepika Padukone's pregnancy and reported Spirit exit to Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar success and Don 3 saga, DeepVeer continue to dominate headlines, debates and social media discourse. Here's why Bollywood's most talked-about couple has become a cultural phenomenon that extends far beyond films.

Dua Lipa, Callum Turner take wedding joy to Sicily's streets after London ceremony Dua Lipa and Callum Turner have kicked off their lavish wedding celebrations in Sicily days after quietly getting married in London. Here's what unfolded at the star-studded welcome party and what comes next.

Janhvi Kapoor can say no to playing a sexualised centrepiece in film Spoiler, sort of: At some point in Peddi, the camera stops staring at Janhvi Kapoor's midriff and finally looks her in the eye. That's also when the film runs out of things for her to do.

Farah Khan quips about Vashu Bhagnani amid his legal row with David Dhawan Farah Khan joked about Vashu Bhagnani while speaking to Varun and David Dhawan in her vlog. The exchange drew notice because Vashu and David are locked in a legal dispute over Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai.

Office Romance review: Jennifer Lopez can do many things. Saving the film isn't oneRelease Date:Fri Jun 05, 2026 Office Romance, starring Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein, released on Netflix with boardroom chaos and a workplace love story. Slick and watchable, the movie, however, never quite finds the charm or chemistry that makes a romcom linger.

Balan The Boy trailer: Mystery and murder haunt a mother's fight to protect her son The trailer of Balan: The Boy offers a chilling glimpse into a mother-son duo caught in a web of secrets, danger and survival. Directed by Chidambaram and written by Jithu Madhavan, the psychological thriller is set for a June 19 release.

general news.headline: Youth won't be puppets: BJP chief jabs Cockroach party for 'negative politics'Taking a swipe at the Cockroach Janta Party, BJP president Nitin Nabin accused people sitting abroad of attempting to drag Indian youth into negative politics.

Air Force to ferry NEET papers for June 21 retest as NTA tightens exam securityThe NTA chief, Abhishek Singh, said the Indian Air Force will transport NEET question papers for the June 21 exam. Authorities were told to tighten security and counter misinformation after the paper leak fiasco led to the cancellation of previous examination which affected over 22 lakh students.

Did Sourav Ganguly ask Yusuf Pathan to quit as MP for Mamata? Dada clears airSourav Ganguly has issued a detailed clarification after a report claimed Mamata Banerjee requested him to nudge Yusuf Pathan to resign as Baharampur MP so that she could contest the ensuing bypoll and enter the Parliament.

Suryakumar Yadav reacts to India's T20I captaincy change: Happy for Shreyas IyerIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav has sent his best wishes to Shreyas Iyer after the Indian selectors named the latter captain of the T20I side. The Ajit Agarkar-led selection panel handed the baton to Shreyas, who has not played a T20I since December 2023.

Did Janhvi Kapoor flag concerns about her Peddi role? Leaked chats go viralAlleged leaked chats circulated by Janhvi Kapoor's fan clubs suggest that the actor had raised concerns about camera angles and the portrayal of her character in Peddi long before the film came under public scrutiny.

Mirra Andreeva ends Maja Chwalinska's dream run, wins maiden Grand Slam titleFrench Open 2026: Mirra Andreeva fulfilled her Grand Slam dream at Roland Garros, defeating Maja Chwalinska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest women's singles champion in Paris since Monica Seles in 1992 and claim her maiden major title.

Domestic LPG price raised by Rs 29 per 14.2-kg cylinder, second increase in three monthsThe latest revision comes as state-owned oil marketing companies continue to struggle with mounting losses on fuel sales. Industry estimates suggest that before the latest increase, companies were losing around Rs 703 on every domestic LPG cylinder sold. Even with the fresh hike, the gap between selling prices and actual costs is not expected to be fully bridged.

Singapore orders YouTube, Facebook, X to block posts targeting Indian communitySingapore has directed social media platforms to disable access to posts undermining its multicultural model. The government said the foreign-sourced content threatened racial harmony and multiculturalism.

Inside the CJP protest: Anger, anxiety and a demand for accountabilityCockroach Janta Party (CJP) held its first protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. While it attracted supporters from traditional left students' outfits, hundreds of "YouTubers" and "influencers", the anger over the NEET-UG leaks and the glitches in the on-screen marking system was evident.

Gill, Rahul deliver dose of Test reality to Afghanistan as India dominateIndia vs Afghanistan 1st Test, Day 1: KL Rahul and Shubman Gill drove India into command against Afghanistan in Mullanpur. Afghanistan's missed reviews, dropped chances and tiring spells left the visitors well behind the hosts early on in the contest.

Iran sets $24 billion 'trust test' for Trump, links it to peace talksIran demanded the release of USD 12 billion in frozen assets immediately after signing an interim agreement with the Trump administration, with another USD 12 billion to be released at a later stage.

What about Obama, Modi? Tharoor counters Ramachandra Guha's Rahul Gandhi criticismShashi Tharoor pushed back against Ramachandra Guha's criticism of Rahul Gandhi's prime ministerial credentials, arguing that leadership is not defined solely by prior ministerial experience.

No more goodbyes: Has Indian cricket finally learned to move on?Handcrafted ByOur Editors He won the Asia Cup. He defended the World Cup. He captained better than almost anyone before him. But Suryakumar Yadav won't get a farewell series. That, increasingly, is the point.

R Praggnanandhaa an incredible fighter: Carlsen praises Norway Chess title winMagnus Carlsen praised R Praggnanandhaa after the Indian Grandmaster made history as the first player from India to win the Norway Chess title.

Monsoon rain to drench South India, heatwave to grip North on SundayAs the southwest monsoon brings heavy rain to the south and northeast, the north continues to reel under intense heatwave conditions.

Cockroach party rally draws students to Jantar Mantar; Wangchuk backs protestWhat began as a satirical online campaign has now spilled onto the streets of the national capital. Students, parents and young professionals gathered at Jantar Mantar as the movement's demands grew louder.

Annamalai's formula is likely to be a blockbuster. Vijay has already proved itActor C Joseph Vijay's rise to power in Tamil Nadu validates much of what K Annamalai propounded but the BJP didn't have the patience for. Here's why we say that Vijay proves that Annamalai's political movement and party might be a superhit in Tamil Nadu.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

USA detains Iraq football team's striker, interrogates him for 7 hoursIraq's World Cup striker Aymen Hussein was held and questioned for nearly seven hours at Chicago's O'Hare Airport. The move has once again put the spotlight on an issue that fans and critics had expressed concerns about in the lead-up to the tournament.

Spurned lover attacks teen with axe, tries to hang her, then throws her from roofGraphic video footage of the incident shows the attacker attempting to hang the bleeding 17-year-old girl from the roof before letting go of the rope, causing her to plunge to the ground below, after which he jumped down and approached her again.

In Army Captain's viral proposal pic a marriage of 50 years no one's talking aboutCaptain Bharat Bhardwaj's proposal beside a Cheetah helicopter drew a barrage of split opinions, but a far more important marriage went unnoticed. India still relies on Cheetah helicopters, the 50-year-old military workhorse with a troubling crash record, even as delays stall the arrival of its replacement.

Why JEE Advanced girl topper Arohi Deshpande avoided the 'topper mindset'JEE Advanced 2026 female topper Arohi Deshpande secured AIR 77 after moving from Pune to Kota for preparation. She credits her parents, teachers and friends for the achievement. Her advice to aspirants is simple: stay relaxed, sleep adequately, stay organised and trust the preparation process.

Chief Justice faces questions on dissent at UK lecture, India calls act indecorousChief Justice of India Surya Kant's lecture at Birkbeck in London was interrupted after attendees raised questions on dissent and his earlier remarks. The disruption drew condemnation from the Indian High Commission and renewed attention on the controversy around his "cockroach" comments.

Mumbai man accidentally pays auto driver Rs 15,682. Then something unusual happensA Mumbai man accidentally transferred Rs 15,682 to an auto driver while rushing to a client meeting. What followed turned a disastrous day into a story he still remembers.

sports.headline: Shubman Gill advised to focus on WTC and ODI World Cup to avoid burnout India's selection committee has asked Shubman Gill to prioritise Test and ODI cricket through the 2027 World Cup cycle, aiming to manage his workload and prevent burnout despite his strong IPL 2026 season.

India outclass Bangladesh to win SAFF Women's Championship after seven years India defeated Bangladesh 3-1 in the SAFF Women's Championship final to clinch a record-extending sixth title, ending a seven-year wait and completing an unbeaten campaign.

USA detains Iraq football team's striker, interrogates him for 7 hours Iraq's World Cup striker Aymen Hussein was held and questioned for nearly seven hours at Chicago's O'Hare Airport. The move has once again put the spotlight on an issue that fans and critics had expressed concerns about in the lead-up to the tournament.

Nothing's come easy for Suryakumar Yadav: Rohit Sharma reacts after captaincy sack Rohit Sharma backed Suryakumar Yadav after his T20I axing, praising his resilience and journey to the top, while also endorsing Shreyas Iyer as India's new T20 captain.

Afghanistan spinners struggled, but head coach sees valuable lessons Richard Pybus said Afghanistan's inexperienced spinners will learn from a bruising day against India in Mullanpur. He pointed to Kharote's character, Malik's no-balls and Rahul's reprieve as key themes of the day.

Mirra Andreeva lifts French Open trophy twice in funny goof-up: Watch French Open 2026: Fresh from winning her maiden Roland Garros title, Mirra Andreeva amused fans with a post-match trophy-lifting mix-up. On Saturday, the 19-year-old beat Maja Chwalinska to claim her maiden Grand Slam title.

Sooryavanshi's childhood coach on India call-up: BCCI have found their poster boy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's childhood coach, Manish Ojha, hailed the 15-year-old's maiden India call-up, saying the BCCI may have found its next poster boy. Congratulating the youngster on his historic selection, Ojha expressed hope that Sooryavanshi would become one of the faces of Indian cricket in the years to come.

Mirra Andreeva ends Maja Chwalinska's dream run, wins maiden Grand Slam title French Open 2026: Mirra Andreeva fulfilled her Grand Slam dream at Roland Garros, defeating Maja Chwalinska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest women's singles champion in Paris since Monica Seles in 1992 and claim her maiden major title.

R Praggnanandhaa an incredible fighter: Carlsen praises Norway Chess title win Magnus Carlsen praised R Praggnanandhaa after the Indian Grandmaster made history as the first player from India to win the Norway Chess title.

Ex-Mumbai cricketer among 25 others provisionally suspended for doping Former Mumbai cricketer Bhupen Lalwani is among 25 athletes provisionally suspended by NADA for doping offences, with national record holder KM Deeksha also featuring on the list.

Suryakumar Yadav reacts to India's T20I captaincy change: Happy for Shreyas Iyer India captain Suryakumar Yadav has sent his best wishes to Shreyas Iyer after the Indian selectors named the latter captain of the T20I side. The Ajit Agarkar-led selection panel handed the baton to Shreyas, who has not played a T20I since December 2023.

50 in 50: Rishabh Pant takes out IPL's anger on Abdul MalikIndia wicketkeeper-batter Rishabh Pant looked in good touch on his return to the Test set-up. Pant scored a fifty in 70 balls in his 50th Test match and remained unbeaten at the end of Day 1 in the only Test match against Afghanistan.

Gambhir's faith gave me freedom to perform: Sai credits India coach for 81 vs AFGSai Sudharsan credited head coach Gautam Gambhir's unwavering backing for giving him the freedom and mental clarity to express himself at the crease after scoring a fluent 81 on the opening day of India's one-off Test against Afghanistan.

Suryakumar will leave behind an MS Dhoni-like legacy as India captain: GavaskarFormer India captain Sunil Gavaskar believes Suryakumar Yadav will leave behind a captaincy legacy similar to that of MS Dhoni, despite losing both the T20I captaincy and his place in India's squad just three months after leading the team to a successful T20 World Cup title defence.

Sooryavanshi to get India debut or warm bench in Ireland? Gavaskar respondsSunil Gavaskar has urged India to play Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in the T20I series against Ireland. He said the 15-year-old's IPL form deserved a reward, even as the senior dressing room itself offers valuable learning.

Suryakumar snub ruthless, will be hard line to follow for selectors: R AshwinFormer India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin described the selectors' decision to drop Suryakumar Yadav from India's T20I squad and remove him as captain as a "ruthless" call, saying it would be a difficult standard to maintain going forward.

PM Modi congratulates Indian women's hockey team after U18 Asia Cup bronzePrime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated the Indian women's hockey team after they secured the bronze medal at the U18 Asia Cup 2026. Modi praised the side's grit and highlighted the growing strength of women's hockey in the country.

Final Highlights: Andreeva wins maiden Slam after beating ChwalinskaFrench Open Women's Singles Final Highlights: Mirra Andreeva has won the Roland Garros title after beating Maja Chwalinska in the final. Andreeva took only an hour and 22 minutes to win the match 6-3, 6-2 on Centre Court.

Gill, Rahul deliver dose of Test reality to Afghanistan as India dominateIndia vs Afghanistan 1st Test, Day 1: KL Rahul and Shubman Gill drove India into command against Afghanistan in Mullanpur. Afghanistan's missed reviews, dropped chances and tiring spells left the visitors well behind the hosts early on in the contest.

Gill carries IPL form into Tests, slams 11th hundred to deflate AfghanistanShubman Gill carried his rich IPL 2026 form into the longest format, bringing up his 11th Test century with a composed and authoritative knock against Afghanistan in the one-off Test.

Vaibhav will win many trophies for India: Proud father reacts to T20I call-upA proud father of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi believes that his son will win a lot of trophies for India in the future. Vaibhav, just 15 years of age, was called up for the Indian T20I team for the series against Ireland and England, as well as the Asian Games 2026.

SL vs IND: Dhruv Jurel named India A captain, Auqib Nabi receives maiden call-upDhruv Jurel will lead India A in two four-day matches against Sri Lanka A, with Jammu & Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi earning his maiden call-up. The tour is expected to serve as a key red-ball preparatory assignment, giving players valuable exposure to Sri Lankan conditions.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group L preview: Will England finally bring the trophy home?England enters a demanding World Cup group under Thomas Tuchel alongside Croatia, Ghana and Panama. Group L will test Tuchel's disputed selection calls against Croatia's pedigree, Ghana's volatility and Panama's rise.

Suryakumar Yadav reacts to new look Indian team and Sooryavanshi call-upSuryakumar Yadav welcomed India's new-look T20I squad for the England and Ireland tour and congratulated Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on his maiden call-up. His message underlined the transition taking shape in India's T20 set-up and the attention on its next generation.

KL Rahul rides his luck to continue fine Test form, scores 12th hundredKL Rahul survived an early reprieve and turned it into his 12th Test hundred against Afghanistan in Mullanpur. Rahul got to his first century as India vice-captain and made Afghanistan pay for their call for not going for a DRS early in his innings.

Virat Kohli ruled out of England ODI tour, Yashasvi Jaiswal named replacementYashasvi Jaiswal has returned to India's ODI set-up after replacing the injured Virat Kohli for the Afghanistan series. The recall puts him back in a crowded 2027 World Cup conversation even as attention stays elsewhere.

Why Rajat Patidar was ignored for India T20I call-up despite back-to-back IPL titlesDespite captaining Royal Challengers Bengaluru to back-to-back IPL titles and enjoying another prolific season with the bat, Rajat Patidar failed to find a place in India's T20I squad for the Ireland and England tour.

Sooryavanshi picked himself: Spellbound selectors hand wonder kid T20I call-upIndia chief selector Ajit Agarkar revealed that they were left spellbound by the abilities of 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Agarkar explained that it was impossible to leave the youngster out of the T20I team after back to back incredible IPL seasons.

Why Suryakumar Yadav was sacked as India's T20I captainIndia chief selector Ajit Agarkar said Suryakumar Yadav's omission from the T20I squad for the tours of Ireland and England was driven by concerns over his form and the team's plans for the next World Cup cycle.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
